title: Zanthoxylum cordobense
taxonomy: Zanthoxylum cordobense belongs to the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the citrus or rue family. It is a genus of flowering plants native to the Americas and parts of Asia. The species Zanthoxylum cordobense is a shrub or small tree that is native to Argentina. The Rutaceae family includes various species of flowering plants, many of which are aromatic and produce berries or fruits. Zanthoxylum cordobense is classified within the Rutaceae family based on its botanical characteristics and morphological features.
uses: Zanthoxylum cordobense is a species of plant native to the mountainous regions of the Cordoba province in Argentina. Unfortunately, information on its medicinal uses is scarce, but it is believed to possess various bioactive compounds with potential health benefits. In traditional medicine, it has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, pain, and inflammation. However, its effectiveness and safety for human consumption have not been extensively scientifically studied or proven. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and potential applications of Zanthoxylum cordobense.
benefits: Zanthoxylum cordobense, also known as Argentine prickly ash, has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its potential health benefits. The plant's leaves and fruits are rich in antioxidants, which may help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation. Its extracts have been studied for their antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiparasitic properties, suggesting its potential use in treating various health conditions. Additionally, the plant's leaves and stems contain compounds that may exhibit anti-diabetic and anti-hyperlipidemic activities. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Zanthoxylum cordobense for medicinal purposes.

parts: Zanthoxylum cordobense, also known as the Argentine prickly ash, is a plant species native to Argentina. The medicinal parts of Zanthoxylum cordobense include its roots, bark, and leaves. These parts have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions. preparations: Zanthoxylum cordobense, a shrub native to Argentina, has been used in traditional herbal medicine.
